questions.question: Explain the concept of friend class in C++ with an example syntax.
keyPoints: Friend class is a class granted special access to private/protected members of another class
Syntax: friend class ClassName; inside the class
The friend class can access all private and protected members directly
Example: class A { friend class B; }; makes B a friend of A

questions.question: List and explain two properties of friendship in C++ that are important to understand.
keyPoints: One-way: Friendship is not mutual - if A declares B as friend, B can access A but not vice versa
Not inherited: Derived classes of friend do not inherit friendship
Not transitive: Friend of friend is not a friend
Location: Declaration can be in any access specifier section

questions.question: What are the advantages and disadvantages of using friend classes in C++?
keyPoints: Advantages: Allows sharing private data between related classes, enables operator overloading, maintains better encapsulation than making all members public
Disadvantages: Creates tight coupling, breaks encapsulation principle, makes code harder to maintain, can lead to misuse if overused

questions.question: Write a C++ program to demonstrate the use of friend class where one class accesses private members of another class.
keyPoints: Create two classes with proper friend declaration
Access private members through friend class member function
Show output demonstrating access to private data
Proper use of header files and main function

questions.question: Explain the difference between friend function and friend class in C++.
keyPoints: Friend function: A single function gets access to private members of a class
Friend class: Entire class gets access to private members
Friend class provides broader access than friend function
Both break encapsulation in a controlled manner

questions.question: Can friendship be inherited or transitive? Justify your answer with reasoning.
keyPoints: No, friendship is NOT inherited - derived classes of friend do not get access
No, friendship is NOT transitive - friend of friend is not automatically a friend
This design maintains encapsulation and prevents unintended access
These restrictions make the friend mechanism more controlled and predictable

questions.question: When is it appropriate to use friend classes in C++? Give suitable scenarios.
keyPoints: When two classes need tight collaboration and share private data
When implementing operator overloading requiring access to private members
When getters/setters would be unnecessarily verbose
In factory patterns or visitor patterns where classes work closely together

questions.question: Explain friend classes in detail with a complete C++ program demonstrating: (i) declaration of friend class, (ii) accessing private members, (iii) one-way nature of friendship, and (iv) what happens when friendship is not inherited.
keyPoints: Complete program with two classes demonstrating friend declaration
Friend class accessing private data members
Demonstration that friendship is one-way (reverse not allowed)
Proof that derived classes do not inherit friendship
Proper explanation of each part
Correct output demonstration

questions.question: Discuss the concept of encapsulation and how friend classes in C++ relate to it. Explain why friendship should be used sparingly with appropriate examples.
keyPoints: Explain encapsulation as OOP pillar promoting data hiding
Friend classes allow controlled access to private members
Discuss how this both supports and challenges encapsulation
Provide examples of appropriate use (operator overloading, tight coupling)
Warn against overuse leading to tight coupling
Suggest alternatives like public getters/setters when appropriate

questions.question: Design a scenario where friend classes are the best solution. Write a C++ program with two classes: a BankAccount class and a Transaction class, where Transaction (as friend) can access private balance of BankAccount to perform deposit and withdrawal operations.
keyPoints: Design BankAccount with private balance and necessary methods
Declare Transaction as friend of BankAccount
Transaction class accessing private balance directly
Implement deposit and withdrawal functionality
Show proper encapsulation with controlled access
Demonstrate working program with sample transactions

questions.question: Explain all properties of friendship in C++ including: (a) one-way nature, (b) not inherited, (c) not transitive, (d) can be declared in any access section. Write programs to demonstrate each property.
keyPoints: Explain one-way nature with example
Explain non-inheritable property with example showing derived class cannot access
Explain non-transitive property showing A's friend of B, B's friend of C doesn't make A friend of C
Explain that friend declaration location doesn't matter
Provide working code examples for each property
